title: phlomis samia subsp. maroccana
intro: Phlomis samia subsp. maroccana, also known as Moroccan Jerusalem sage, is a subspecies of the Phlomis samia plant, native to the mountainous regions of North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Phlomis samia subsp. maroccana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and provide relief from anxiety and stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
